---
title: "Metal Fuels Agglomeration"
subtitle: "Agglomeration dynamics and diagnostics"
author: "Ivo Roghair"
date: "18 June 2026"
format: tue-revealjs
tue-cover-image: assets/tue-bg-img1.jpg
tue-cover-logo: assets/tue-logo-white.png
# Red bar opacity (0 = fully transparent, 1 = fully opaque)
tue-cover-bar-opacity: 0.65
# Slide background colour — used for normal slides and the bottom footer strip of the cover slide
tue-slide-bg: "#f3efef" 
# Content slide text padding (top/bottom left/right), e.g. "30px 58px"
tue-content-slide-padding: "30px 58px"
# Short title shown on the left of the footer bar on every content slide
tue-short-title: "Ivo Roghair — Agglomeration in Metal Fuels Reduction"
# Logo shown on the right of the footer bar (defaults to TU/e scarlet logo)
tue-footer-logo: "assets/tue-logo-wide-scarlet-alpha.png"
---

## Motivation

Metal fuel particles can agglomerate under high-temperature conditions, which changes the effective surface area, reaction rate, and transport behavior.

- This starter project uses a widescreen Reveal.js deck.
- The TU/e-inspired cover slide is generated automatically from the document metadata.
- Replace the cover image path or logo path in the front matter when you want a different opening slide.

## Example Analysis

:::: columns
::: {.column width="58%"}
### Key points

- Agglomeration changes the particle size distribution.
- Optical diagnostics and post-processing can be summarized on one slide.
- Equations render with standard LaTeX math support.

$$
\tau = \frac{d_p^2}{18 \nu}
$$
:::

::: {.column width="42%"}
![](assets/rightpic.jpg){fig-alt="Sample image" width=80%}
:::
::::

## Roadmap

1. Experimental setup
2. Image-based agglomerate metrics
3. Model comparison and sensitivity

## Next Steps

1. Replace the title, subtitle, author, and date in the front matter.
2. Swap `tue-cover-image` for your own high-resolution title visual when needed.
3. Add new slides with `##` headings and standard Quarto Markdown.
